evals.prompt: Check my inbox and show me the 10 most recent emails.
evals.expected_output: Uses superhuman inbox command (or mcp__superhuman__superhuman_inbox). Shows results in a structured list. Does not fabricate emails. Uses correct CLI syntax if generating a bash command.

assertions.name: uses-superhuman-inbox
assertions.description: Response uses superhuman inbox command or the Superhuman MCP tool, not Gmail directly or a generic approach

assertions.name: limit-is-10
assertions.description: Limit parameter is set to 10 or --limit 10

assertions.name: does-not-fabricate-emails
assertions.description: Response does not invent example emails - it calls the tool and shows real results or explains it would

evals.prompt: Search my emails for anything from the Endsight team in the last 2 weeks and give me a summary of what's been discussed.
evals.expected_output: Uses superhuman search with appropriate query (from:endsight or from:endsight.com). Reads relevant threads. Summarizes actual content found. Correct CLI syntax used.

assertions.name: uses-superhuman-search
assertions.description: Uses superhuman search command with from: filter for Endsight

assertions.name: reads-thread-content
assertions.description: After finding threads, reads them with superhuman read to get actual content before summarizing

assertions.name: summary-based-on-actual-content
assertions.description: Summary is based on thread content retrieved, not fabricated
